title: lastrea columbiana
intro: Lastrea columbiana, also known as the Black Fern, is a type of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae and is native to the mountainous regions of Central and South America. The medicinal properties of Lastrea columbiana include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of consuming Lastrea columbiana include its potential to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support immune function, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. Herbal preparations of Lastrea columbiana can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and are often used in traditional medicine to treat a variety of ailments.
